violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety that is registered with the RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Licensed Food Safety Manager for the location. Application printed and supplied at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area (top of small prep unit) with no lid/straw. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Beverage was relocated at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

Several food items set for hot holding in the hot hold cabinet were observed held at 117-120 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, food items had been placed in the cabinet earlier in the morning hours. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

Several food items prepared on site were observed not date marked with the date of preparation or not re-marked with a 6-day use-by date from the day it was defrosted as day 1. Food items prepared on site are to be properly marked with a use-by date that is not to exceed 7 days unless frozen (Prep date is day 1, defrost date is day 2). Prep/Thaw dates were verified at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

A container of sauce was observed held beyond 7 days from the date of preparation. Sauce was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of inspection, the establishment was out of replacement sanitizer. PIC to use the 3 bay sink for proper sanitizing until sanitizer can be replaced. (Per the PIC, will obtain same day.) New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

At the time of inspection, several food items in the walk-in cooler were observed held at 44 to 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC called for refrigeration technician to adjust cooler same day as inspection. Food item observed at 46 degrees Fahrenheit was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

At the time of inspection, several food items were observed not marked with the proper date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Per the PIC and employee, the food items were prepared within 7 days. Food items were properly dated at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

The knives at the prep units are stored on an unclean surface (slid between prep unit edges) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Knives were properly cleaned and moved to clean surface at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

At the time of inspection, the Walk-in cooler was observed in need of repair/adjustment. The walk-in cooler ambient temperature was observed at 42 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures observed maintained at 43 to 46 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC placed call for adjustment at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2024

At the time of inspection, the 3 bay sink was observed to have a pre-flush sprayer only. The Faucet is lacking a spout that is large enough to reach and fill all three bays effectively for washing/rinsing/sanitizing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2024
